feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Custom Fields Failing to Influence Procurement Pricing (PPR0) in Intercompany Scenarios
entry.content:
We have created custom fields in PO item, these custom fields fail to influence the Procurement (MM) pricing (Condition Type: PPR0). 

We have maintained condition records for PPR0 using a combination that includes our custom fields .

During PO creation, the system recognizes the condition type PPR0 but shows that the custom field values are "Initial" or "Not Found" in the Pricing Analysis, thus failing to fetch the correct price.

And the BADI: MM_PUR_S4_PO_DEFINE_CNDNCTRL has already been implemented.

 

However, even after manually updating the conditional price, it cannot be passed into the price communication.

We cannot find the configuration path to enable this specific OData source or to map these custom fields to the Procurement Communication Structure (KOMP).

We request assistance in enabling as valid pricing influence factors for PO.

entry.title: Cascading Stock Transfer Between MRP Areas and Two Central Warehouses Without Daisy-Chaining
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m designing a replenishment logic in SAP S/4HANA for multiple maintenance stations, each represented as an independent MRP Area (Type 02).

Scenario:

  • Each station = separate MRP Area.
  • Two central warehouses: WH1 and WH2.

Target Logic:

  1. If the station stock is depleted → check WH1.
  2. If WH1 is empty → check WH2.
  3. If WH2 has stock → trigger a direct stock transfer reservation/order from WH2 to the station.

Key Requirement:
Avoid “daisy-chaining” (WH2 → WH1 → Station). The transfer should come directly from the warehouse that has stock.

What I’ve tried:

  • Special Procurement Keys (SPK): with fixed issuing storage locations.
  • Quota Arrangements (MEQ1): attempted priority-based “if-then” logic, but handling stock-location priorities across warehouses within the same plant is challenging.

Question:
Can standard MRP handle this dynamic “WH1 first, then WH2” logic without manual intervention or custom BAdI coding? How should Source Determination and SPKs be configured to enable automatic switching between warehouses based on stock availability?

Any guidance or examples from similar implementations would be highly appreciated!

Best regards,

Markus

entry.title: Automatic batch determination with releasing the production order
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am working on S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition and trying to set up automatic batch determination for components when a production order is released.

In the material master (MRP 2 view), the field 'Batch Entry' only offers value 3 – 'Batch Determination at Order Release', but not value 4, which  triggers the batch determination fully automatically in the background during order release.

With value 3, it seems the system only checks whether a batch determination is expected, but does not execute it automatically – meaning the user still has to manually click the 'Batch Determination' button in the component list of the production order.

My questions:
1. Is value 4 intentionally not available in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition (Clean Core / reduced scope)?
2. Is there any standard way to trigger batch determination fully automatically upon production order release in the Public Cloud, without manual user interaction?
3. Or is the recommended SAP Best Practice in the Public Cloud to perform batch determination at the time of goods issue rather than at order release?

Any experience or guidance from the community would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance.

Jana

entry.title: Add custom field in the Project Control - Project Briefs - Milestones section
entry.content:
User would like to add a required custom field under the Milestones section within Project Briefs app, while the enhancement spot to implement this change is not identified yet.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: [S/4HANA Cloud] Even after deleting the parent item's planned order, running MRP does not delete the
entry.content:
Even after manually deleting the parent item's planned order and running MRP, the planned orders for the component items are not deleted.
Regarding the MRP execution parameters, only the parent item is specified. Do I also need to specify the component items?
Planning mode: 3 Delete/Re-register.

entry.title: In the interface for creating a purchase order, is there a profitability field under account assignm
entry.content:
Due to analytical requirements, we need the accounting documents generated after purchase order creation to be associated with dimensions such as profit center, project, and customer. For this purpose, we configured the profitability field under the account assignment tab of the purchase ord er in the system, and after testing with a goods receipt, the resulting accounting document did contain the data we needed. However, the interface documentation does not include the profitability field in the purchase order interface. May I ask whether the standard interface for purchase orders simply does not have a profitability field?

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb9f1469daf04bd894ab2167f8132a1a/c89eec80ec2043d980cb7b8c89e0a00a.html?locale=zh-CN&q=%E5%8F%91%E7%A5%A8API

entry.title: Payment strategy Payment API
entry.content: Does the APP for managing payment applications have an API or background process corresponding to the "pay" button in the payment strategy? Finance - China Payment Requisition Integration (SAP_COM_0727) This SAP_COM_0727Communication Scenario only includes the options of submission, approval, and rejection. However, there is no payment operation API interface without a payment strategy. Could you provide the API for this interface or tell me how to carry out the back-end operations for this payment strategy?

entry.title: How to handle subcontracting procurement between group companies?
entry.content:
There are two companies, A and B, both under the same group. Company A is a trading company. A receives returned products that need repair, but A does not have the repair capability and needs to hand them over to B for repair. During the repair process, raw materials are required. The raw materials are at Company B, and A does not provide them. Company B also needs to use labor. The raw material and labor costs consumed by B during the repair process need to be borne by Company A.

My current idea is for Company A to issue a subcontracting purchase order, but I am not sure if there is a better solution. May I ask whether any engineer has designed a similar scenario?

entry.title: Deactivate the use of Reference Exchange Rate Indicator in Group Reporting
entry.content:
Hello, 

we are setting up the Group Reporting solution for our client. We use the Translation Method S0905. The Reference Exchange Rate Indicator used is C. 

Regarding translation of Local Currencies to the Group Currency, we want the B/S items to be translated using the CLOsing Exchange Rate, and the P/L items via the AVERage Exchange Rate. 

PROBLEM: When running the Currency Translation task within the Data Monitor app, we receive CTA (Currency Translation Adjustments) differences created for P/L FS items (posted to the 314800 FS Item). It is because the Reference ER is based on the CLOsing ER and the P/L FS items are based on the Average ER. 

This problem does not occur for B/S Items because it is based on the CLOsing ER (like the Reference ER). We want to achieve the same thing with the P/L items. 

Our client does not need to use the Reference Exchange Rate. This means, we don't want any CTA to be created neither for B/S nor P/L FS items. Is there any way to deactivate the Reference ER functionality? 

Or is the special FS Item 314800 can be hidden from the reporting? 

Many thanks in advance!! I will reward kudos to helpful solutions

entry.title: Unable to Assign Collection Management Attributes via API in S/4HANA Public Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

We are currently working on an integration between our S/4HANA Public Cloud system and a Microsoft Dynamics 365 system for customer creation and updates.

As part of this integration, we are using the API_BUSINESS_PARTNER inbound service along with its related entities such as A_BusinessPartner, A_BusinessPartnerAddress, A_BusinessPartnerContact, A_BPRelationship, A_BPContactToAddress etc

During the process, we are creating the following roles for each customer:

  • FLCU00 (Customer – Financial Accounting)
  • FLCU01 (Customer – Sales Area)
  • UDM000 (Collections Management role)

While the roles are successfully created, we are facing challenges with Collections Management data. Specifically, we are unable to assign the following attributes to the customer via the integration:

  • Collection Segment
  • Collection Group
  • Collection Strategy
  • Collection Specialist

We have not been able to identify any API or entity within the Business Partner service that supports maintaining these collections-related attributes.

During our analysis, we found several SAP-delivered jobs, such as:

  • Assignment of Collection Profiles to Business Partners
  • Changing Segment Data
  • Replacing Collection Specialists

However, these jobs do not fully meet our requirements. For example, the Assignment of Collection Profiles to Business Partners job requires a fixed “Created On” date parameter. This makes it difficult to automate daily processing, as we cannot dynamically define a rolling date range (e.g., “today” or “today - 1”) when scheduling the job.

As a result, we are struggling to design an automated solution that ensures Collections Management attributes are consistently assigned to newly created customers.

We would greatly appreciate any guidance on:

  • Available APIs or extensibility options to maintain Collections Management data
  • Recommended best practices for automating this process in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Any alternative approaches that others have successfully implemented

Our goal is to ensure that our Accounts Receivable team can effectively use the Process Receivables app with complete and accurate customer data, including assigned collection profiles and contacts.

SAP Integration Suite SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA business partner #

entry.title: WT Creation Issue in S/4HANA Public Cloud EWM – Foreground vs Background Behavior
entry.content:
Guys, I am facing an issue related to WT creation in S/4HANA Public Cloud EWM.

What I did was: I created a custom storage type, defined a Putaway Control Indicator (PCI), and maintained the Storage Type Search Sequence (STSS). I also assigned the STSS to the storage type and mapped the PCI with the STSS using the configuration node “Specify STSS in SIMG.”

Then, I assigned the PCI to the product master and posted a GR. When I tried to create a WT for putaway, the system created the WT with a standard bin for putaway (S970 – clarification zone bins).

However, when I created the WT using the “Change Inbound Delivery – Deliveries” app, the system created the WT for my custom bin.

In simple terms, foreground WT creation (via Fiori app) is working as expected, but background WT creation (via Warehouse Monitor) is not.

The same issue occurs for outbound as well. I created an activity area and assigned it to the storage type, defined a stock removal indicator and assigned it to the product master, and also maintained the storage type search sequence with the removal indicator.

This setup works in the “Manage Outbound Delivery Orders” app, but not in the Warehouse Monitor, where it shows the bin determination error mentioned below.

I hope I made my problem statement clear. Waiting for your responses

entry.content:
How to edit item text in customer line item?

entry.title: Configuration for document type for Bank statements (MT940) upload
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Can you please guide us with the configuration path for the configuration of 'document type' of the Bank statements (MT940) imported for BRS.
Currently the document type for the documents generated is 'SA'.

entry.title: Transportation Zone field needs to be add in condition table field catalog
entry.content:
Transportation Zone is not available in the field catalog of SD condition tables, preventing its use in access sequences and pricing.

Details

  • Standard field Transportation Zone (KNA1‑LZONE) is not visible in the condition table field catalog.
  • Attempted to publish a custom field to use Transportation Zone for pricing, but system throws an access sequence error.
  • KNA1‑LZONE appears to be commented/inactive in Eclipse, causing technical limitations.

Requirement

  • Create a Sales Order Header custom field populated automatically from Customer Master → General Data → Transportation Zone.
  • Make this field available in field catalog, condition tables, and access sequence.
  • Maintain pricing via VK12 to enable Transportation Zone–based automatic pricing.

entry.title: Migration Cockpit
entry.content:
We want to move the G/ L Account data from Parallel Customizing to Parallel Test with the Migration Cockpit App, can you please give us a step by step guidance how that works

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: New depreciation key customizing option not available
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We are trying to create new depreciation key in SAP system . However, we could not find any customizing options.

Please review SAP system and provide us option to customized new depreciation key in SAP system.

 

Thanks & Regards,

Vibhor Bansal

entry.title: Catching Bounce Mails
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

we are currently looking for a way to check for bounce mails after sending invoices to customers to ensure, that the mail addresses of the reciepients are up to date. 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Apply Customer Down Payment in Debit Memo Request
entry.content:
We have the requirement to manually created debit memo requests / debit memos and clear these against previously created down payments. 

During the process of creating the debit memo request, the existing down payments are not automatically proposed. We can manage this manually. But never the less we have the need to show on the output for the debit memo, that the existing down payment will be applied and therefore no further payment is needed. 

Is there an option to achieve this. On the DMR I did not find any options to:

  • refer to the existing down payment
  • use a condition type to enter the applied down payment amount
  • anything else. 

Please propose how to handle this requirement. 

Thanks in Advance

Johannes

entry.title: Chart of Accounts in Parallel Line - Test environement (SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 2602)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I hope you are doing well.

We are currently working on a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition greenfield implementation and we are facing an unexpected behavior related to the Chart of Accounts.

In the main line, we modified the Chart of Accounts in the Customizing tenant (creation of new G/L accounts using the copy functionality and updates of descriptions). All these changes correctly generated transport requests.

We then created a branch to copy these changes to the Customizing tenant of the parallel line. After the branch, we noticed that:

- All Chart of Accounts changes are visible in the parallel line Customizing tenant
- However, no transport requests exist in that tenant

We are now facing an issue because we want to perform SIT in the parallel line test tenant, but the Chart of Accounts (and some related configurations) are not available in that system.

Has anyone faced a similar issue, or are there any guidelines on how this situation should be handled in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Any help would be highly appreciated.

Kind regards,
Alexandre B
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance

entry.title: External Billing API – credit memo equivalent of EOX1/EDX1/CIX1?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We use BillingDocumentRequest_In (SAP_COM_0120, scope 1Z6) in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to create billing documents from an external source system, without any preceding sales order in S/4HANA.

The invoice case works with EOX1 / EDX1 / CIX1.

For credit memos we need the equivalent combination but BillingDocumentType G2 with EOX1 / EDX1 fails with VF 002 (“Cannot bill SD documents of type EOX1 using billing type G2”), and we cannot find a delivered EOXC / EDXC or any other external credit-memo document type in customizing.

Please let us know:
  • Which SalesDocumentType, SalesDocumentItemCategory and BillingDocumentType should be sent in BillingDocumentRequest_In to create a credit memo without a preceding sales order (i.e. the counterpart of EOX1 / EDX1 / CIX1)?
  • If no such combination is delivered, what is the supported alternative for posting external credit memos in Public Cloud?
